@@ Official GitHub permission reference:
 | Scheduling | Reminders, agent-invoke tasks, worker queue processing, and cron-backed automation |

@@ release-notes/v0.1.8.md
+## Changes
+
+- Formalized `v0.1.7` as the single long-term baseline for future one-click upgrades
+- Marked the temporary bridge line around `v0.1.6` as withdrawn so the updater no longer treats it as an officially supported path
+- Improved upgrade-path messaging for installs that are already on a withdrawn release line
+
+## Notes
+
+- Future official one-click upgrades now continue from `v0.1.7`
+- Older recovery lines such as `v0.1.4` and `v0.1.6` are no longer part of the maintained upgrade path
+- Installs still on a withdrawn line should recover by redeploying from the current `stable` branch
+

@@ That means:
 
 If you later want one release to support multiple source versions, make sure the expected file bases really line up for every listed source tag. `previous_supported_tags` only controls path selection; it does not magically make one patch set fit incompatible source trees.
 
+Current project policy:
+
+- `v0.1.7` is the long-term supported baseline for future one-click upgrades
+- older bridge releases such as `v0.1.4` and `v0.1.6` are treated as withdrawn recovery lines
+- do not keep extending multiple historical bridge branches unless user volume justifies the complexity
+
 ## Required environment variables

 That install button should point to the `stable` branch so first-time users always install the latest formal release, not an in-between development snapshot from `main`.
 
+For ongoing upgrades, treat `v0.1.7` as the long-term supported baseline. If you are still on an older withdrawn bridge line, the recommended recovery path is a fresh redeploy from `stable` instead of trying to keep chaining one-click upgrades forever.

@@ Right after you confirm that dialog, SEAJelly now shows a second completion dial
 
 If this install is several formal releases behind later on, `Dashboard -> Updates` now catches it up one official release hop at a time instead of trying to apply a risky cross-version patch in one jump.
 
+Today, SEAJelly's long-term managed-update baseline starts at `v0.1.7`. Earlier withdrawn bridge releases may show a warning in `Dashboard -> Updates` and are better handled by redeploying the latest `stable` build.
+

notes_md:
## Changes

- Formalized `v0.1.7` as the single long-term baseline for future one-click upgrades
- Marked the temporary bridge line around `v0.1.6` as withdrawn so the updater no longer treats it as an officially supported path
- Improved upgrade-path messaging for installs that are already on a withdrawn release line

## Notes

- Future official one-click upgrades now continue from `v0.1.7`
- Older recovery lines such as `v0.1.4` and `v0.1.6` are no longer part of the maintained upgrade path
- Installs still on a withdrawn line should recover by redeploying from the current `stable` branch
